title: Web Security Threats and Defenses
text: Web Security Threats and Defenses Threats to web applications can take many forms, and affect multiple areas of the application. The topics in this section describe those threats in more detail and suggest ways in which you can defend against them. Table 1. Threats Affecting Application Areas Common Threats Application Area Buffer overflow attacks Data I/O for Web Applications Logging and Auditing Web Applications Denial of Service Attack Logging and Auditing Web Applications Cross-Site Scripting Authentication for Web Applications Browser Security Data I/O for Web Applications Session Management for Web Applications Data Leaks Logging and Auditing Web Applications Data Leaks Browser Security Eavesdropping Browser Security Web Application Configuration and Deployment Identity Spoofing Authentication for Web Applications Interpreter Injection Data I/O for Web Applications Man-In-The-Middle Attacks Authentication for Web Applications Web Application Configuration and Deployment Password Cracking Authentication for Web Applications Web Application Configuration and Deployment Session Fixation Authentication for Web Applications Session Management for Web Applications Session replay Data I/O for Web Applications Session Sidejacking Authentication for Web Applications Session Management for Web Applications Access to confidential data Data manipulation Execution of unauthorized commands Privilege escalation Authorization for Web Applications Cookies Containing Sensitive Data Session Management for Web Applications Was this topic helpful?
